volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 834 - Section 965)

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: τόδʼ ἔτι βρέφος
sections.translation: I left you still a baby ,

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: ἔλιπον ἀγκάλαισι νεαρὸν τροφοῦ
sections.translation: young in the arms of your nurse , young in the house . O my soul , you have been more fortunate than words can say .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: πέρα καὶ λόγου πρόσω τάδʼ ἐπέβα .
sections.translation: I have come upon things that are beyond wonder , far from speech .

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: τὸ λοιπὸν εὐτυχοῖμεν ἀλλήλων μέτα .
sections.translation: For the rest of time , may we be fortunate with each other !

sections.label: Section 842
sections.original: ἄτοπον ἁδονὰν ἔλαβον , ὦ φίλαι ·
sections.translation: O my friends , I have found an extraordinary joy ; I am afraid that he will fly from my hands into the air .

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ἰὼ Κυκλωπὶς ἑστία · ἰὼ πατρίς ,
sections.translation: O Cyclopean hearths ; o my country , dear Mycenae , I thank you for his life , for his nourishment , because you brought up this light of the house , my brother .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: γένει μὲν εὐτυχοῦμεν , ἐς δὲ συμφοράς ,
sections.translation: We are fortunate in our family , but in our circumstances , my sister , we were born to be unfortunate in life .

sections.label: Section 852
sections.original: ἐγᾦδʼ ἁ μέλεος , οἶδʼ , ὅτε φάσγανον
sections.translation: I was unhappy , I know , when my wretched father put the sword to my throat .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: οἴμοι . δοκῶ γὰρ οὐ παρών σʼ ὁρᾶν ἐκεῖ .
sections.translation: Alas ! Though I was not present , I seem to see you there .

sections.label: Section 856
sections.original: ἀνυμέναιος , ὦ σύγγονʼ , Ἀχιλλέως
sections.translation: O brother , when I was brought , not a bride , to the treacherous bed of Achilles ;

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: παρὰ δὲ βωμὸν ἦν δάκρυα καὶ γόοι .
sections.translation: but beside the altar there were tears and wails . Alas for the libations there !

sections.label: Section 862
sections.original: ᾤμωξα κἀγὼ τόλμαν ἣν ἔτλη πατήρ .
sections.translation: I also mourned for the daring act of our father .

sections.label: Section 864
sections.original: ἀπάτορʼ ἀπάτορα πότμον ἔλαχον .
sections.translation: Fatherless was the fate I received , fatherless .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἄλλα δʼ ἐξ ἄλλων κυρεῖ
sections.translation: One thing comes from another , by divine fortune .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: εἰ σόν γʼ ἀδελφόν , ὦ τάλαινʼ , ἀπώλεσας .
sections.translation: Yes , if you had killed your brother , unhappy one !

sections.label: Section 868
sections.original: ὦ μελέα δεινᾶς τόλμας . δείνʼ ἔτλαν
sections.translation: O wretched , in my dreadful daring !

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: δείνʼ ἔτλαν , ὤμοι σύγγονε . παρὰ δʼ ὀλίγον
sections.translation: How dreadful were the things I endured , alas , my brother ! By only a little you escaped an unholy death , slain by my hands . But how will these things end ? What fortune will assist me ?

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: τίνα σοι πόρον εὑρομένα —
sections.translation: What way will I find to send you from this city , from slaughter , to your native Argos ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: πρὶν ἐπὶ ξίφος αἵματι σῷ πελάσαι ;
sections.translation: before the sword draws near to your blood ? This is your business , unhappy soul , to find out . On the dry land , not in a ship ?

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ἀλλὰ ποδῶν ῥιπᾷ
sections.translation: But if you go on foot , through trackless paths and barbarian tribes , you will draw near to death .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: στενοπόρου πέτρας μακρὰ κέλευθα να-
sections.translation: But through the dark rocks of the narrow passage is a long way for flight by ship .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: τάλαινα , τάλαινα .
sections.translation: I am unhappy , unhappy !

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: τίς ἂν οὖν τάδʼ ἂν ἢ θεὸς ἢ βροτὸς ἢ
sections.translation: Who , either god or mortal or something unexpected , might accomplish a way that is no way , and reveal a release from troubles for the only two children of the house of Atreus ?

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: ἐν τοῖσι θαυμαστοῖσι καὶ μύθων πέρα
sections.translation: It is marvelous and surpasses a fable , this event that I myself have have seen and shall relate , not as hearsay .

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: τὸ μὲν φίλους ἐλθόντας εἰς ὄψιν φίλων ,
sections.translation: When friends come into the sight of friends , Orestes , it is reasonable to embrace ; but we must leave off wailing and turn to other matters :

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ὅπως τὸ κλεινὸν ὄνομα τῆς σωτηρίας
sections.translation: how we shall get the glorious name of safety and leave the foreign land . For wise men take opportunities , and do not overstep their fate to get other pleasures .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας · τῇ τύχῃ δʼ οἶμαι μέλειν
sections.translation: Well said ; but I think fortune will take care

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: τοῦδε ξὺν ἡμῖν · ἢν δέ τις πρόθυμος ᾖ ,
sections.translation: of that with us ; when one is eager , divine strength is likely to be greater .

sections.label: Section 912
sections.original: μηδέν μʼ ἐπίσχῃ γʼ · οὐδʼ ἀποστήσει λόγου ,
sections.translation: ( to Pylades .) Let nothing hold me back ; nor will it prevent me speaking before I first find out Electra ’ s fate , for you are all dear to me .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: τῷδε ξυνοικεῖ βίον ἔχουσʼ εὐδαίμονα .
sections.translation: She lives with this man , ( pointing to Pylades ) and has a happy life .

sections.label: Section 916
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ ποδαπὸς καὶ τίνος πέφυκε παῖς ;
sections.translation: What country is he from , and who is his father ?

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: Στρόφιος ὁ Φωκεὺς τοῦδε κλῄζεται πατήρ .
sections.translation: Strophius of Phocis is the name of his father .

sections.label: Section 918
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ἐστί γʼ Ἀτρέως θυγατρός , ὁμογενὴς ἐμός ;
sections.translation: He is related to me , by the daughter of Atreus ?

sections.label: Section 919
sections.original: ἀνεψιός γε , μόνος ἐμοὶ σαφὴς φίλος .
sections.translation: He is a cousin , and my only true friend .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: οὐκ ἦν τόθʼ οὗτος ὅτε πατὴρ ἔκτεινέ με .
sections.translation: He was not born when my father tried to kill me .

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: οὐκ ἦν · χρόνον γὰρ Στρόφιος ἦν ἄπαις τινά .
sections.translation: He was not ; for Strophius was childless for some time .

sections.label: Section 922
sections.original: χαῖρʼ ὦ πόσις μοι τῆς ἐμῆς ὁμοσπόρου .
sections.translation: Welcome , husband of my sister !

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: κἀμός γε σωτήρ , οὐχὶ συγγενὴς μόνον .
sections.translation: And also my savior , not only a relation .

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: τὰ δεινὰ δʼ ἔργα πῶς ἔτλης μητρὸς πέρι ;
sections.translation: But how did you dare that dreadful deed with our mother ?

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: σιγῶμεν αὐτά · πατρὶ τιμωρῶν ἐμῷ .
sections.translation: Let us be silent on that ; I was avenging my father .

sections.label: Section 926
sections.original: ἡ δʼ αἰτία τίς ἀνθʼ ὅτου κτείνει πόσιν ;
sections.translation: What reason did she have to kill her husband ?

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: ἔα τὰ μητρός · οὐδὲ σοὶ κλύειν καλόν .
sections.translation: Let our mother ’ s affairs be ; nor is it good for you to hear .

sections.label: Section 928
sections.original: σιγῶ · τὸ δʼ Ἄργος πρὸς σὲ νῦν ἀποβλέπει ;
sections.translation: I am silent ; does Argos now look to you ?

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: Μενέλαος ἄρχει · φυγάδες ἐσμὲν ἐκ πάτρας .
sections.translation: Menelaus rules there ; I am an exile from my country

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: οὔ που νοσοῦντας θεῖος ὕβρισεν δόμους ;
sections.translation: Our uncle has surely not maltreated our afflicted house , has he ?

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ Ἐρινύων δεῖμά μʼ ἐκβάλλει χθονός .
sections.translation: No , but fear of the Furies drives me out of the land .

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἆρʼ ἐπʼ ἀκταῖς κἀνθάδʼ ἠγγέλης μανείς ;
sections.translation: That was the madness that they reported there on the shore ?

sections.label: Section 933
sections.original: ὤφθημεν οὐ νῦν πρῶτον ὄντες ἄθλιοι .
sections.translation: That was not the first time that I was seen to be wretched .

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: ἔγνωκα · μητρός σʼ οὕνεκʼ ἠλάστρουν θεαί .
sections.translation: I know ; the goddesses were driving you for the sake of your mother .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ὥσθʼ αἱματηρὰ στόμιʼ ἐπεμβαλεῖν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: So as to put a bloody bit in my mouth .

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: τί γάρ ποτʼ ἐς γῆν τήνδʼ ἐπόρθμευσας πόδα ;
sections.translation: Why have you made a journey to this land ?

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: Φοίβου κελευσθεὶς θεσφάτοις ἀφικόμην .
sections.translation: I have come at the commands of Phoebus ’ oracles .

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσειν ; ῥητὸν ἢ σιγώμενον ;
sections.translation: To do what ? Can you speak of it , or must you be silent ?

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: λέγοιμʼ ἄν · ἀρχαὶ δʼ αἵδε μοι πολλῶν πόνων .
sections.translation: I will tell you ; this is the beginning of my many troubles .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τὰ μητρὸς ταῦθʼ ἃ σιγῶμεν κακὰ
sections.translation: When my mother ’ s evil deeds , that I cannot speak of , came into my hands , I was driven to flight by the Furies ’ pursuit ; then Loxias sent me to Athens , to stand trial with the goddesses who may not be named .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ἔστιν γὰρ ὁσία ψῆφος , ἣν Ἄρει ποτὲ
sections.translation: For there is a holy tribunal there , which Zeus once established for Ares , when his hands were stained with blood-pollution . I came there . . . at first , no host would willingly take me in , as one hated by the gods ; then some who felt shame offered me a table apart , as a guest ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: παρέσχον , οἴκων ὄντες ἐν ταὐτῷ στέγει ,
sections.translation: themselves being under the same roof , and in silence they kept me from speaking , so that I might be apart from them in food and drink , and into each private cup they poured an equal measure of wine and had their delight .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: κἀγὼ ʼξελέγξαι μὲν ξένους οὐκ ἠξίουν ,
sections.translation: And I did not think it right to blame my hosts , but I grieved in silence and seemed not to know , while I sighed deeply , that I was the murderer of my mother . I hear that my misfortunes have become a festival at Athens , and they still hold this custom

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: χοῆρες ἄγγος Παλλάδος τιμᾶν λεών .
sections.translation: and the people of Pallas honor the cup that belongs to the Feast of Pitchers . When I came to the hill of Ares to stand my trial , I took one seat , and the eldest of the Furies took the other . I spoke and heard arguments on the murder of my mother ,

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: Φοῖβός μʼ ἔσῳσε μαρτυρῶν , ἴσας δέ μοι
sections.translation: and Phoebus saved me by bearing witness ; Pallas counted out equal votes for me ; and I went away victorious in my ordeal of blood . Some of the Furies who sat there , persuaded by the judgment , marked out a holy place for themselves beside this very tribunal ;
